---
description: "Use when adding, editing, or translating dictionary keys in en.json or de.json, or when updating the Dictionary TypeScript type. Covers the two-locale requirement, type sync, server-only loader, and prop-drilling access pattern."
applyTo: "src/dictionaries/**"
---

# i18n Dictionary Conventions

This project uses a custom static JSON-based i18n system with two locales: **English (`en`)** and **German (`de`)**.

## Golden Rule

**Always update both `en.json` AND `de.json` in the same change.** Never add a key to one file without a matching key in the other. Missing keys cause runtime errors — there is no fallback.

## File Locations

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/dictionaries/en.json` | English strings |
| `src/dictionaries/de.json` | German strings |
| `src/lib/types/dictionary.ts` | TypeScript type derived from the dictionary shape |

## Adding a New Key

1. Add the key at the correct nesting level in `src/dictionaries/en.json` with the English string.
2. Add the **same key path** in `src/dictionaries/de.json` with the German translation.
3. If the new key introduces a new interface or shape, update `src/lib/types/dictionary.ts` accordingly.

## Type Sync

`src/lib/types/dictionary.ts` defines the `Dictionary` type and all nested interfaces (`QuizSectionDict`, `QuizCommonDict`, `QuizResultLevel`, etc.). When adding new top-level sections or changing the structure, update the types to match.

## Access Pattern

- The dictionary is **loaded server-side only** via `getDictionary(lang)` in `src/app/[lang]/dictionaries.ts`.
- Pass `dict` (or the relevant sub-object) as props to child components.
- **Never** import `getDictionary` or the JSON files in client components — the loader is marked `server-only`.
- **Never** use React Context or a global store to expose the dictionary — prop-drill it.

## Quiz Dictionary Structure

Each quiz section lives under `dict.quiz.<quizKey>` and must match `QuizSectionDict`:
```
metaTitle, metaDesc, title, subtitle, description,
estimatedTime, questionCount,
categories: { [categoryId]: label },
questions:  { [questionId]: { text, hint? } },
results:    { low, medium, high } each with { narrative, actions[] }
```
